name: Arthur Fiedler
abstract: Arthur Fiedler (December 17, 1894 – July 10, 1979) was an American conductor known for his association with both the Boston Symphony and Boston Pops orchestras. With a combination of musicianship and showmanship, he made the Boston Pops one of the best-known orchestras in the United States.

concerts.label: Evening at Carnegie Hall to Benefit Music for the Blind

works.t: Piano Concerto No. 1 in B-flat Minor, Op. 23: 3. Allegro con fuoco

about_quotes.speaker: Maxine Sullivan
about_quotes.evidence: I remember watching Arthur Fiedler's Christmas Show a couple of years ago

about_quotes.speaker: Louie Bellson
about_quotes.evidence: I played in a couple of symphonies... Fiedler made a motion, but he followed us. I gave him a lot of respect for that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Louie Bellson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Schuller Gunther
about_quotes.evidence: He studied with Bill Z[?], who was one of the great players in the [Boston] Pops Orchestra for years and years with Arthur Fiedler
about_quotes.doc_title: Schuller Gunther — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Akiyoshi Toshiko
about_quotes.evidence: I had to play for Arthur Fiedler in daytime... Arthur Fiedler said, “You shouldn‟t be so like that...”
about_quotes.doc_title: Akiyoshi Toshiko — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Sauter
about_quotes.evidence: I was there in case Fiedler wanted to ask a question... he begins to read what I have on the sheet and all I had written down was just a guide, but he played what I wrote.
